description: Config for scripts/check-doc-drift.mjs — codifies the Class #11 (multi-occurrence fix completeness) sweep pattern. Each entry is one drift CHECK. Adding a new check is a config edit, not a code edit: append a new object with { id, kind, title, sources?, targets?, patterns?, allowlist?, hint }. The script registers a small set of 'kind' handlers (source-vs-target-coverage, forbidden-pattern-in-targets, self-consistency) and dispatches each entry to the matching handler. New drift CLASSES (entirely new kinds of comparison) need both a config entry AND a new handler; new INSTANCES of an existing class are config-only.

checks.title: All error codes documented
checks.description: Every error.code value emitted from packages/**/lib/errors.js (including STATE_MACHINE_<KIND> expansions) must appear in the error-codes handbook page.
checks.hint: Document the missing error code in site/src/content/docs/handbook/error-codes.md following the existing format (### `CODE_NAME`, Class, Trigger, Message shape, Hint, Operator action).

checks.title: All STATUS.* enum values documented
checks.description: Every status string in the STATUS object exported from packages/dogfood-swarm/db/schema.js must be mentioned somewhere in the state-machines handbook page. The wave classifier states (new/recurring/fixed/unverified/...) and finding-review states (candidate/reviewed/accepted/...) are the public vocabularies operators need named.
checks.hint: Mention the missing status in site/src/content/docs/handbook/state-machines.md. The state-machines page documents PUBLIC state vocabularies (record classification, finding review, wave classification). Internal control-plane plumbing (run/wave/agent_run statuses) is exempt via skipKeys in scripts/doc-drift-patterns.json. If a finding-review or wave-classifier state is genuinely internal-only and shouldn't be documented, add it to the allowlist with a comment explaining why.

checks.title: No legacy paths in user-facing docs
checks.description: User-facing docs (handbook + docs/) must not contain legacy paths. The README and CLAUDE.md retain explicit historical references on purpose (per CLAUDE.md 'Working with the legacy' section).
checks.hint: Replace the legacy reference with the canonical equivalent (dogfood-lab/testing-os, packages/<name>/, repo-relative path). Historical context belongs in README.md / CLAUDE.md / HANDOFF.md, NOT in user-facing handbook pages.

checks.title: No version-specific terms that should be parameterized
checks.description: Narrative docs should not hardcode counts that move between releases (e.g. '9-Phase' when the protocol is now 10 phases). Code snippets, CHANGELOG, and the version-stamp block in README are exempt.

patterns.label: stale 9-Phase reference (protocol is 10-phase since Stage D added)

checks.hint: Update the count to the current value. If you need to refer to a historical state, scope the reference (e.g. 'pre-Stage-D, the protocol was 9 phases') rather than asserting the current count is wrong.

checks.title: Every opening code fence has a language tag
checks.description: Stage D wave 23 (D-CI-001 / F-827321-010): every ```fence in site/src/content/docs/handbook/*.md must declare a language so Starlight's Shiki applies syntax highlighting, the copy-button, and consistent border treatment. Bare ``` openers render as plain monospace and read as draft-quality next to tagged blocks. Use ```text for ASCII / CLI output / state diagrams, ```bash / ```yaml / ```json / ```ts for code. The handler walks each target line-by-line, toggling an in-fence flag, and only flags openers — closing fences are correctly bare.
checks.hint: Add a language tag to the opening fence: ```text for ASCII diagrams or CLI output, ```bash for shell commands, ```yaml / ```json / ```ts / ```js for code, ```mermaid if/when the Mermaid integration lands. Closing fences stay bare.

checks.title: PROTOCOL.md stage references are internally consistent
checks.description: PROTOCOL.md mentions Stages A–D in the Health Pass plus Phase 5–8 (Feature), Phase 9 (Final), Phase 10 (Full Treatment). Every Stage and Phase referenced in narrative must also be defined as a section/header. Drift here means the title or summary moved without the body following.

must.label: Stage D must be defined with 'Visual' lens
Stage D must appear as a bolded entry in the stage list

checks.hint: Update PROTOCOL.md so the title block, the stage list, the per-phase audit lens enumeration, and the coordinator checklist all refer to the same set of stages. Class #11 sweep pattern: fix every occurrence in one pass, never one-by-one.
